conbody.p: Follow these rules when creating a password:

ul.li: Choose a password that is easy for you to remember, but difficult for others to guess. Avoid using your name or the names of family members, familiar dates, such as birthdays, or common words, such as password. Pass-phrases can also be used.

The password must comply with the following rules that define a strong password. It must be between 8 and 128 characters in length and must include at least three out of the following four criteria:
Upper case letter
Lower case letter
Number
Special character
Apart from the above rules, the password cannot be same as previous 10 passwords that you had set.
Passwords are case sensitive. For instance, Pb2Ag and pb2ag are different passwords. To make your password more secure, use a mixture of uppercase and lowercase letters.
